reason: Values are uniform over [0,100] and well-spread, so a sample mean of ~50.82 is consistent with the data.
Among y_pred=1 rows the sample has many 0,1 false positives, implying precision ~0.6, far below the claimed 0.8817.
Sample shows abundant 0,1 false positives and 1,0 false negatives, giving F1 ~0.6 rather than the claimed 0.7656.
Residuals are commonly ~1-2 against a target std of ~9, implying R2 ~0.99, not the claimed 0.999.
Per-row absolute errors average roughly 1.2-1.4, well above the claimed MAE of 0.5602.
Score shows almost no separation between y_true classes (many true=1 with low scores, true=0 with high), implying AUC near 0.5, not 0.7913.
